howItWorks.intro: This paper weight converter uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: GSM from Bond: GSM = Bond Weight × 3.76
GSM from Cover: GSM = Cover Weight × 2.708
GSM from Index: GSM = Index Weight × 1.81
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What does GSM mean for paper?
faq.answer: GSM stands for grams per square meter. It's the international standard for measuring paper weight. Standard copy paper is 75-90 GSM, and business cards are typically 300-350 GSM.

faq.question: What is the difference between bond and cover weight?
faq.answer: Bond weight uses a 17×22 inch basis size (for writing papers), while cover weight uses a 20×26 inch basis size (for cardstock). 80lb cover is much heavier than 80lb bond.

faq.question: What paper weight should I use for business cards?
faq.answer: Business cards are typically 300-350 GSM (110lb cover or 80lb cover minimum). Thicker stock (400+ GSM) feels more premium.

faq.question: Is the Paper Weight Converter free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Paper Weight Converter?

No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds.
